message:
test(ft-xbnl0.2.4): metrics server mid-flight cx-cancel contract (tick 322)

Added `metrics_server_start_with_cx_mid_flight_cancel_stops_accept_loop`
— pins that cancelling the cx *after* the metrics server has started
causes the accept loop to exit without requiring the shutdown flag.

The metrics `start_with_cx` clones the Cx into the spawned accept-loop
task and checks `task_cx.is_cancel_requested()` at the top of each
iteration. Two shutdown signals exist side-by-side — the cx and the
atomic shutdown flag — and this test pins that the cx path alone is
sufficient to terminate the loop.

Why this matters: an operator cancelling on program termination (e.g.
via a signal handler) cannot reliably flip the shutdown flag but will
naturally cancel the ambient request cx. If a regression removed the
cx check from the accept loop, the server would only stop via the flag
path — the operator-cancel case would hang forever.

The test starts the server, cancels the cx without touching the flag,
and asserts the handle completes within 2s (generous over the 250ms
accept-poll interval). A post-assertion sanity check confirms the
shutdown flag remained false, proving the cancel took effect via the
cx path.

Complements:
- Pre-cancel refuses to bind (`...pre_cancelled_refuses_to_bind`)
- Pre-cancel wait returns fast (`...wait_with_precancelled_cx...`)
- Happy path (`...start_with_cx_happy_path...`)

Test result: ok. 1 passed; 0 failed
Compile time: 1m 12s cold.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
